text: Cheap CPU proxy (no finetune): the late abstract-b*c band is NOT product-general -- on DeepSeek-Math-7B L23 (the genuinely staged band, aggregate d=+0.59) same-subproduct profile similarity is graded by PRODUCT MAGNITUDE (corr +0.83): large products 10-20 sit well above the diff baseline (r 0.39-0.63) while small products 3/4/5/8 sit AT/BELOW it (r 0.02-0.09), with 40% of products below baseline and a permutation heterogeneity p<5e-5. Positive magnitude grading replicates at every model/layer (Qwen-7B/1.5B, +0.21..+0.64). This is a necessary-condition proxy, not the discriminator: it predicts the band would FAIL on held-out (especially small/novel) products -- favouring product-keyed lookup over a clean rule -- but the definitive FT held-out test (a-0010/e-0008) is still unrun.
